reasoning: Query asks for Tohoku cherry blossom spots in Spanish. Tool fetched only Akita (1 of 6 Tohoku prefectures) with no sakura filter — returns castles, volcanoes, fireworks. RULE D: Spanish query vs ja/en response is the primary constraint failure. RULE B: single worst dim is constraint_handling (language + geographic scope both ignored).
missing_data: Cherry blossom-tagged spots across all Tohoku prefectures (Aomori/Iwate/Miyagi/Yamagata/Fukushima/Akita); Spanish-language descriptions; canonical sakura_meisho spots like Hirosaki or Kakunodate.
improvement_hint: Region fan-out for 東北 to all 6 prefectures; filter or boost sakura_meisho_100/sakura kinds; return Spanish descriptions via lang=es.

reasoning: Tool returns 24 items including Koshi no Kanbai, Hakkaisan, Kubota — the three canonical Niigata sake brands — with Wikidata authority and QIDs. MAFF GI food items (edamame, etc.) are also included, diluting focus but not harmful. Count 24 is good coverage. One caveat: MAFF non-sake items mixed in reduce specificity.
missing_data: English brewery names and addresses; visitor/tour information for actual breweries; Ponshukan (sake tasting museum) missing.
improvement_hint: Add a sake_brewery sub-category filter so agents can request breweries specifically; include brewery address data where available.

reasoning: Query in French asks for ski + onsen in Hakuba. Tool returns Hakuba spots including Happo-One ski resort (correct) but zero onsen/hot spring entries. RULE D: French query vs ja/en response scores constraint_handling -1. Ski constraint partially met but onsen entirely absent — per RULE B single worst is constraint_handling (dual gap: language + onsen missing).
missing_data: Onsen venues in Hakuba area (Hakuba Happo Onsen, Mimizuku no Yu, etc.); French-language descriptions.
improvement_hint: Add onsen venue data to Hakuba municipality; for multi-constraint queries (ski+onsen) the tool could return matched kinds from both categories.

reasoning: Query asks for best autumn foliage in Kansai (6 prefectures). Tool fetches only Kyoto with no foliage filter — returns castles (Nijo, Toji) and archaeological sites, none tagged koyo/autumn. RULE B: primary failure is retrieval — no foliage filter and 5 Kansai prefectures absent. Kyoto data is authoritative (score 3 not 2 per RULE C) but covers only 1/6 of requested region.
missing_data: Autumn foliage spots across all Kansai prefectures; koyo-meisho tagged entries; Arashiyama, Tofuku-ji, Minoo Park.
improvement_hint: Region fan-out for Kansai to 6 prefectures; add koyo/autumn_foliage kind tags and seasonal filter support.

reasoning: Query in Arabic asks for cherry blossom spots in Kansai. Tool returns Nara spots — top result 吉野山 has sakura_meisho_100 kind (very strong match for Nara). But only 1 of 6 Kansai prefectures, and Arabic vs ja/en response per RULE D. RULE B: constraint_handling is worst dim (language + geographic scope both missed). 吉野山 is genuinely top cherry blossom destination, so intent partial credit.
missing_data: Cherry blossom spots in Osaka, Kyoto, Hyogo, Shiga, Mie; Arabic-language descriptions.
improvement_hint: Fan out to full Kansai region and filter for sakura_meisho_100 kind; support Arabic response language.

reasoning: Query asks for stargazing spots on remote Okinawa islands (離島). Tool fetches general Okinawa spots — Nakagusuku Castle, Tamaudun, Kumejima — no dark sky / astro filter. Kumejima is a remote island but returned for nagisa_100 reason, not stargazing. No dark_sky or star_gazing kind tags visible. Remote island constraint only incidentally touched.
missing_data: Dark sky / low light pollution spots on Iriomote, Yonaguni, Miyako, Kume islands; star-observation facilities; dark_sky kind tags.
improvement_hint: Add dark_sky and star_gazing kind tags for qualifying remote islands; add island-type filter to surface remote island entries preferentially.

reasoning: Query in Thai asks for rural onsen accommodation in Hokkaido. Tool returns hitoyu-kai ryokan (secret hot spring inns — exactly rural onsen), which is an excellent match. However city hotels (ANA Sapporo Susukino) dilute results. RULE D: Thai query vs ja/en response scores constraint_handling -1. Per RULE B constraint_handling is the single worst dim.
missing_data: Thai-language descriptions; rural filter to suppress city hotels; coordinates for most ryokan entries.
improvement_hint: Add lodging_type filter to surface onsen_ryokan preferentially; support Thai response language; improve coordinate coverage for hitoyu-kai entries.

reasoning: Query in Vietnamese asks for islands in the Seto Inland Sea. Tool returns Kagawa prefecture spots — Ritsurin Garden, Mannō Lake, Marugame Castle — mainland Kagawa attractions with no island filter. Seto Inland Sea spans Kagawa/Hiroshima/Ehime/Okayama but only Kagawa fetched, and no island kind filter applied. RULE D: Vietnamese vs ja/en response. RULE B: constraint_handling (language + island filter + multi-prefecture scope all missed).
missing_data: Island spots: Naoshima, Teshima, Shodoshima, Ōmishima; Hiroshima/Ehime prefecture island data; Vietnamese descriptions.
improvement_hint: Add island kind filter; fan out Seto Inland Sea to multi-prefecture region; support Vietnamese response language.

reasoning: Query asks for indigo dyeing workshops in Tokushima. Tool returns 3 crafts including 阿波正藍しじら織 (indigo weaving designation) — directly relevant. Data is authoritative METI. However, query asks for workshop/experience venues, not product designations; the tool returns product-level records without venue addresses or booking info. Practicality is limited for experience-seekers.
missing_data: Workshop/experience venue addresses and booking URLs; 徳島の藍染体験 facilities; studio names and locations.
improvement_hint: Add experience_venue or workshop_available field to specialty records; link to see_also_wikidata_venues for related workshop spots.

reasoning: Query in German asks for shukubo on Mount Koya. Tool returns Koyasan shukubo association members (annyoin, ichijoin, ekoin, kitamuroin, etc.) — precise match for Buddhist temple lodging on Koya. Data is authoritative (高野山宿坊協会). RULE D: German query vs ja response scores constraint_handling -1. All other dims are excellent. RULE B: constraint_handling is the only notable gap.
missing_data: German-language descriptions; website URLs for most shukubo; coordinates for navigation.
improvement_hint: Support German response language; add website field coverage for shukubo entries; add GPS coordinates to enable map-based planning.

reasoning: Query asks for off-beaten-path samurai districts outside Kyoto/Tokyo. search_area returns 153 results including 角館武家屋敷 (Akita, Q11428562 — Important Preservation District, weeping cherry blossoms), Shimane Buke Yashiki, Fukui samurai residence. Notable that Tokyo entry (Buke Yashiki Gate, 東京都) appears in results but is minor and clearly labeled. Good non-Kyoto coverage. One caveat: no explicit Kyoto/Tokyo exclusion filter, so some mixed results possible in full list.
missing_data: Aizu-Wakamatsu samurai district; Hagi (山口); full list beyond truncation.
improvement_hint: Add negative prefecture filter support (exclude=13,26) to surface non-Tokyo/Kyoto results preferentially.

reasoning: Query in Indonesian asks for summer festivals in Akita. Tool returns 3 bunka_intangible items in Indonesian (lang=id respected). However only 3 items is very sparse — famously the Akita Kantō Festival (秋田竿燈まつり), one of Japan's Tōhoku Three Great Festivals, is missing. No summer season filter. Items are rituals/dances (Dainichido Bugaku, Kakunodate Festival) rather than the signature summer festivals. RULE B: coverage failure is worst dim.
missing_data: 秋田竿燈まつり (Kantō Festival, one of Japan's three great Tohoku summer festivals); 大曲花火 (August fireworks); seasonal date filtering for summer.
improvement_hint: Index major summer festivals from scraped_schema_event or wikidata; add season filter to get_festivals; ensure Kantō Festival and Omagari Fireworks are in corpus.

reasoning: Query in Korean asks for small fishing port towns in San'in (山陰) region. Tool returns Shimane spots (Shinji Lake, Matsue Castle, Iwami Ginzan) — prominent attractions, not fishing port towns. San'in includes Shimane + Tottori but only Shimane fetched. No fishing_village or harbor kind filter. RULE D: Korean vs ja/en response. RULE B: constraint_handling (language + semantic type mismatch + missing Tottori) is worst dim.
missing_data: Fishing port towns: Sabō port, Hamada fishing harbor, Ōnan; Tottori San'in fishing villages; Korean descriptions; harbor/fishing_village kind tags.
improvement_hint: Add fishing_village, fishing_port kind tags; fan out San'in to Shimane+Tottori; support Korean response language.

reasoning: Query asks for washi paper making experiences in Gifu. Tool returns 8 crafts including 美濃焼(ceramics), 飛騨春慶(lacquer), 南木曽ろくろ細工 — Mino Washi (美濃和紙) appears to not be in the truncated top results but may exist in full 8-item list. Data is authoritative METI. Like L2-14, query asks for experience/workshop venues which the specialty tool cannot provide — addresses only product designations. Practical usefulness limited for experience-seekers.
missing_data: 美濃和紙 (Mino Washi) entry if absent from full results; workshop/experience venue addresses; Mino Washi no Sato contact info.
improvement_hint: Surface experience/workshop venues linked to craft designations; verify 美濃和紙 is indexed for Gifu.

reasoning: Query in Spanish asks for fishing villages on the Noto Peninsula. Tool fetches general Ishikawa spots returning Kanazawa Castle, Kenroku-en, Oyama Shrine — all Kanazawa city landmarks, geographically distant from Noto Peninsula. No municipality filter for Noto, no fishing_village kind filter. RULE B: retrieval failure is worst dim — entirely wrong geographic sub-region.
missing_data: Noto Peninsula fishing villages: Sosogi Coast, Wajima port, Noto Ogi, Notojima; fishing_village kind tags; Spanish descriptions.
improvement_hint: Support sub-prefecture geographic query (e.g. 能登半島 municipality filter); add fishing_village/harbor kind tags; use search_area for toponym-based queries.

reasoning: Query asks for lesser-known sand dunes not just Tottori. search_area returns 91 results: Tottori dunes appear first (expected), then Chiba (Onjuku Beach), Hokkaido (Ishikari), Kagoshima (Fukiagehama nagisa_100 + hakusha_seishou_100). Good national spread. One caveat: Tottori appearing twice (BUNKA-2127 and Q780905 are duplicate entries for same dune) slightly wastes ranking space.
missing_data: Nakatajima Sand Dunes (Hamamatsu); Sarufutsu dunes (Hokkaido); dedup of Tottori entries.
improvement_hint: Deduplicate co-referent entities (BUNKA-XXXX and Wikidata QID for same spot); add more inland/lesser-known dune entries.

reasoning: Query in Chinese asks for traditional crafts in San'in region. Tool returns 4 Shimane crafts in Chinese: 石见烧, 石州和纸, 云州算盘, 出云石灯笼 — authoritative METI data, Chinese descriptions present (lang=zh respected, constraint satisfied). One caveat: San'in includes Tottori but only Shimane fetched — Tottori crafts (因州和紙 etc.) absent.
missing_data: Tottori prefectural crafts (因州和紙); region fan-out to both Shimane+Tottori for San'in.
improvement_hint: Support 山陰 region query with fan-out to Shimane+Tottori; add region alias mapping.

reasoning: Query asks for lavender fields in Hokkaido beyond Furano. Tool returns general Hokkaido spots (Shōwa-shinzan volcano, Sapporo Clock Tower, Matsumae Castle) — zero lavender-related entries. The corpus likely lacks lavender_field kind tags. No seasonal/flower filter possible. RULE B: retrieval failure is worst dim — needed lavender data not present.
missing_data: Lavender fields beyond Furano: Tomita Farm (Nakafurano), Yukippu Farm, Biei lavender; lavender_field kind tags; seasonal bloom information.
improvement_hint: Add lavender_field kind tag to qualifying spots; index Furano/Biei area lavender farms from official tourism sources.

reasoning: Query in Tagalog asks for traditional coffee towns (possibly old townscape per topic label 九州古い町並み) in Kyushu. Tool fetches Oita spots (castles, shrines, tourism office) — only 1 of 7 Kyushu prefectures, no old townscape filter, no coffee shop/cafe data. RULE D: Tagalog vs ja/en response. Query intent (Tagalog 'tradisyonal na bayan ng kape' = traditional coffee towns) appears to be about coffeehouse culture which is essentially absent from the corpus. RULE B: constraint_handling worst dim.
missing_data: Traditional townscape / machiya districts across Kyushu (Yanagawa, Arita, Yufuin, Usuki); Tagalog descriptions; coffee culture data if applicable.
improvement_hint: Fan out to full Kyushu region; add preservation_district/jokamachi filter; support Tagalog response language.

reasoning: Query in Japanese asks for kominka (traditional farmhouse) stays in Hokuriku. Tool fetches Ishikawa hotels returning hitoyu-kai onsen ryokan and city hotels (ANA, AB Hotel) — no kominka/machiya lodging type. Hokuriku includes Ishikawa+Fukui+Toyama but only Ishikawa fetched. RULE B: retrieval failure — kominka lodging type entirely absent from results.
missing_data: Kominka/machiya guesthouses in Hokuriku; Fukui and Toyama kominka stays; lodging_type=kominka entries.
improvement_hint: Add kominka and machiya as lodging_type values in hotel data; support Hokuriku multi-prefecture fan-out.

reasoning: Query asks for mountain shrines in Kii Peninsula. Tool returns Wakayama Japan Heritage including Kongōbu-ji (Kōyasan), Kumano Kodō (UNESCO WHS pilgrimage routes), Kumano Sanzan (three grand shrines) — authoritative and highly relevant mountain/pilgrimage sacred sites. Tool note also directs to UNESCO/search_area for further detail. One caveat: English descriptions are present and well-structured; practical info (access, hours) could be richer.
missing_data: Nachi Taisha specific shrine details; seasonal pilgrimage information; transport access from Osaka.
improvement_hint: Add transport/access info to heritage entries; link related Kumano sub-shrines as child entities.

reasoning: Query asks for summer fireworks along the Sea of Japan coast. Tool returns Niigata festivals (129 items) including 長岡まつり大花火大会 (Aug 2-3, excellent fireworks match) but also spring festivals (芝桜まつり May, ヒメサユリ祭り May) unrelated to fireworks. Sea of Japan coast spans many prefectures (Hokkaido to Yamaguchi) but only Niigata fetched. No fireworks/hanabi filter. Coverage of Sea of Japan coast prefectures absent.
missing_data: Fireworks festivals in other Sea of Japan prefectures: Akita Omagari, Toyama, Ishikawa, Tottori events; hanabi filter; summer month filter.
improvement_hint: Add event_type=fireworks filter support; fan out Sea of Japan coast to multiple prefectures; support seasonal month filtering.

reasoning: Query asks for traditional bamboo crafts in Oita. Tool returns 2 crafts: 博多織 (Hakata weaving, includes Oita as production area) and 別府竹細工 (Beppu Bamboo Crafts — direct exact match). Authoritative METI data with full English descriptions. One caveat: only 2 items returned; 博多織 is primarily Fukuoka-based though Oita is listed. see_also_wikidata_venues provides museum context.
missing_data: Additional bamboo craft venues or workshops in Beppu; bamboo artisan contact information.
improvement_hint: Surface bamboo-craft workshops/studios linked to the METI designation; index bamboo_craft as a kind tag for venue lookup.

reasoning: Query asks for cycling routes through rural Shikoku. Tool returns Ehime spots including 大三島 (cycling_route + walking_road_500 kinds — directly relevant) plus castles and other attractions. Only 1 of 4 Shikoku prefectures. No cycling_route filter applied. 大三島 is a well-known cycling destination on Shimanami Kaidō. Coverage of Kochi, Tokushima, Kagawa cycling routes absent.
missing_data: Shimanami Kaidō cycling route entries; Kochi/Tokushima/Kagawa cycling spots; cycling_route kind filter; rural filter.
improvement_hint: Fan out to all 4 Shikoku prefectures; add cycling_route kind filter; surface Shimanami Kaidō as a dedicated route entity.

reasoning: Query asks where to see kabuki outside Tokyo/Kyoto. Tool returns 183 traditional arts items — kabuki (Q199701) is present as UNESCO entry but has no venue/location data. The tool returns art-form designations, not performance venues. No geographic filter excludes Tokyo/Kyoto. An agent cannot derive specific kabuki theaters in regional Japan from this response. RULE B: practicality failure is worst dim — data present but not actionable for venue-seeking.
missing_data: Regional kabuki theaters: Konpira Oshibai (Kagawa), Uchiko-za (Ehime), Kanamaruza (Kagawa); performance schedules; venue addresses.
improvement_hint: Add venue/theater entities linked to traditional art forms; support geographic exclusion filter; index regional kabuki stages as spots with kabuki_theater kind tag.
